selected_important_turn.text: so let me suggest a split, I get two water and two food, you get one food, one water and three firewood
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first concrete allocation proposal from you and it strongly shifts the interaction from information-sharing/cooperation to a self-favoring, zero-sum framing. It determines how scarce, high-priority items (water, food) will be split and therefore sets the bargaining anchor and trajectory.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The proposal takes more of your top priorities (water and food) despite the opponent having expressed clear needs for food and some water. It misses an integrative opportunity: the opponent has plenty of firewood, so a package trade (more firewood for more of one scarce item) could produce a higher joint outcome and feel fairer. As phrased, it risks signaling opportunism and reduces trust.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Instead of staking out a self-heavy split, open with a proposal that trades on the opponent's surplus (e.g., offer them extra firewood in exchange for the water you need) or ask for their thoughts before specifying numbers. Frame the first offer as exploratory ('one option is...; how does that sound?') to keep cooperation and invite tradeoffs.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: Likely increase if you open tradeoffs (more integrative outcomes); staying as-is keeps joint gain limited.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: Improve if you propose a trade that respects opponent priorities; current wording lowers perceived fairness.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: Increase if you invite input or tradeoffs; current move may make agreement slower or require more concessions.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: Improve if you avoid a self-favoring anchor and show willingness to trade; current move risks eroding trust.

value2reason.Medium: I have a large ground of people, so we need a lot of food
value2reason.High: because I have to stay hydrated because it is hot

value2reason.Low: Firewood can be a struggle to carry around during the day and on hot nights.
value2reason.Medium: Without water, a person could die from dehydration out in the wilderness.
value2reason.High: Without food, a person could die from hunger out in the wilderness.

dialogue.text: hello, how are the preparations coming along?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Hi There, I'm doing okay with my preparations but I am worried that I don't have enough food or water for the next few days.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I also worried about those two items, especially water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Yes, they say it supposed to be really hot the next few days.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: yes, how do you suggest we share these resources?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Tell me, how stocked up are you on food and firewood?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I have firewood, what I need mainly is water, then food
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ah, I see. I have plenty of firewood as well but mostly need food and some water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: so let me suggest a split, I get two water and two food, you get one food, one water and three firewood
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think that's a fair split. Let me confirm: you get two water, two food, and one firewood, while I get one water, one food, and two firewood?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think we can do better by trading some firewood for more water and food. How about I give you two firewood packages in exchange for one more water and one more food? That way, we both get what we need more.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think that trade could work. Let me confirm: you get two water, two food, and one firewood, while I get one water, one food, and two firewood. Deal?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
